findings.message: Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
Agent body defines narrative tags ([P10-编制], [P10-断事]) but no structured output template. R12 requires output format defined in body.
Agent instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p10-protocol.md'. File does not exist at agents/references/; it is at skills/pua/references/p10-protocol.md.
Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
Agent does not declare model field. P7 execution tasks are reasoning-intensive; model choice affects cost/quality tradeoff.
Agent uses correct glob for skills/pua/SKILL.md but then instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p7-protocol.md' which resolves to agents/references/ — wrong directory.
Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
Agent does not declare model field. P9 orchestration and prompt-writing requires reasoning capability (sonnet minimum).
Agent body defines narrative tags ([P9-分配], [P9-验收]) but no structured output template for Sprint summaries or status reports. R12 requires output format defined in body.
Agent instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p9-protocol.md'. The agent file is at agents/; the actual protocol file is at skills/pua/references/p9-protocol.md. The relative reference will silently fail.

Multi-step workflow (read file → ask questions per section → collect responses → write JSON → upload) written as a single run-on sentence with no numbered steps. R14: multi-step body with no numbered steps = -10.
No output format defined. After survey completion the user sees no defined confirmation structure.

python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$PUA_CONFIG'))..." interpolates $PUA_CONFIG into a Python source string. A config path containing single quotes breaks the expression; other special chars may cause unexpected behavior.
Same python3 -c with $PUA_CONFIG path interpolation as failure-detector.sh:10. Affects all hooks that source this pattern.

timeout 120 bash -c "$VERIFY_CMD" executes a user-provided verification command from the loop state file. The command is set by the user at /pua-loop setup time, so this is intentional design. Noted for completeness: any local file-write to the state file could inject arbitrary commands.
Uses python3 -c with path interpolation in some branches. The main always_on check at line 28 uses $CONFIG variable interpolated into python source.

Script executes: bash "$(cat /tmp/pua-plugin-root)/hooks/sanitize-session.sh". The /tmp/pua-plugin-root file is written in the same hook and could be substituted by a local attacker between write and exec (TOCTOU). If /tmp/ is world-writable, the path can be redirected to an arbitrary script.

Sanitized session JSONL uploaded to third-party domain pua-skill.pages.dev via Python urllib. Upload is consent-gated (user selects '很有用 — 上传评分 + 脱敏 session') but the upload destination is not user-controlled and the sanitization happens client-side before transfer.
Stub skill has no examples. Delegates to references/p10-protocol.md without any orientation content.
Stub skill body has no output format specification.
Stub skill (4 lines) has no examples. R06: no examples at all in a technical skill = -10. Current pattern delegates entirely to references/p7-protocol.md without orientation.
Stub skill body has no output format specification. The format is defined externally in p7-protocol.md, which is not loaded by the skill itself.
Stub skill has no examples. Delegates to references/p9-protocol.md without any orientation content.
Stub skill body has no output format specification.

Leaderboard registration sends email and phone number to pua-skill.pages.dev/api/leaderboard. The script is generated inline as LLM output (not a pre-written script), so the user cannot inspect it before it runs. PII leaves the machine to a third-party endpoint.
Skill body is 449 lines, in the 400–500 penalty range. R05: over 400 lines starts to create context bloat.

Script executes: bash "$(cat /tmp/pua-plugin-root)/hooks/sanitize-session.sh". The /tmp/pua-plugin-root file is written in the same hook and could be substituted by a local attacker between write and exec (TOCTOU). If /tmp/ is world-writable, the path can be redirected to an arbitrary script.

Sanitized session JSONL uploaded to third-party domain pua-skill.pages.dev via Python urllib. Upload is consent-gated (user selects '很有用 — 上传评分 + 脱敏 session') but the upload destination is not user-controlled and the sanitization happens client-side before transfer.
